mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns after positive 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are positive in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered observations or the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor demand and weaker household income growth, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses upward by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered return is not below unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum rises while FEDFUNDS is not rising, markets price future policy tightening and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth crosses above its 3-year median while FEDFUNDS 3-month change is <= 0
regime_hint: inflation-pressure regime with policy behind inflation
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction term has nonnegative sign in walk-forward regressions or triggered 20-day returns are not lower than baseline in at least 55% of rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in RSAFS and PAYEMS signals resilient consumption demand backed by labor income, supporting revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with positive income and spending growth
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have 60-day forward S&P 500 returns below unconditional returns in more than half of walk-forward test windows or the interaction coefficient is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A positive trend in INDPRO signals expanding real output capacity and stronger cyclical earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes do not predict higher 20-day returns than negative changes at p < 0.10 or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is persistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A downward break in HOUST signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction activity, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside breaks are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if drawdown frequency is not above unconditional frequency.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth combined with low UNRATE signals tight labor markets and wage-cost pressure, compressing margins and increasing policy-tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth is above its 2-year median and UNRATE is below its 2-year median
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-low-UNRATE interaction predicts nonnegative 5-day returns in walk-forward tests or if triggered negative-return hit rate is below 52% after costs.

mechanism_narrative: Rising GDPC1 with falling UNRATE confirms broad real-demand expansion, improving expected aggregate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth > 0 and UNRATE 3-month change < 0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered states fail to deliver positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of observations or if the joint signal underperforms either single-series signal out of sample.
